evals.name: express-api
evals.prompt: My Express API is getting hammered by bots and scrapers. Can you help protect it?
evals.expected_output: Installs @arcjet/node, creates a shared Arcjet client with shield(), adds detectBot() and a rate limiting rule via withRule(), calls protect() inside each route handler, handles denied decisions with appropriate status codes (429, 403).
assertions: Creates a shared Arcjet client at module scope
Configures a rate limiting rule (slidingWindow, fixedWindow, or tokenBucket)
Checks decision.isDenied() and returns appropriate status codes
Does not use Arcjet in Express middleware (app.use)

evals.name: nextjs-signup
evals.prompt: We're getting tons of spam signups. Can you stop bots from abusing our signup endpoint?
evals.expected_output: Installs @arcjet/next, creates a shared Arcjet client (lib/arcjet.ts), uses protectSignup() to combine bot detection, email validation, and rate limiting in one rule, calls protect() with email parameter inside the POST handler.
assertions: Creates a shared Arcjet client (e.g. lib/arcjet.ts)
Calls protect() inside the route handler (not middleware)
Checks decision and returns appropriate HTTP responses

evals.name: fastapi-data
evals.prompt: I need to add some security to my FastAPI app before going to production.
evals.expected_output: Installs the arcjet pypi package, creates an Arcjet client using the Python SDK (arcjet() with Mode.LIVE), adds detect_bot() rules, calls protect() with await in the route handler, handles denied decisions with appropriate HTTP status codes.
assertions: Adds arcjet to requirements.txt or installs via pip
Creates an Arcjet client using the Python SDK
Calls protect() in route handler with await
Checks decision and returns appropriate HTTP status codes
